content: Safety alignment for large autonomous agents can be reframed as a problem of guiding intrinsic curiosity, rather than just restricting harmful exploration: curiosity-driven exploration methods like ICM and RND that reward agents for exploring uncertain states can be repurposed to penalize curiosity about unsafe, high-risk states that misaligned agents may otherwise spontaneously seek out. This turns the intrinsic motivation mechanism that drives autonomous exploration into a built-in safety guardrail that aligns with human value constraints.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
